text:
Epilepsy Centre 

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 05 July 2018 

Dr Patel  
St George's University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust  
Department of Neurology, Blackshaw Road, London SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Patel,  

Liam Carter, DOB: 14-11-2006, Hospital No: H392748 NHS No. 8475102369  
58 Willowbank Avenue, Surbiton, KT6 5HR  

Diagnoses: 

Epilepsy (type not specified). 

I reviewed Liam Carter today in routine follow‑up with her mother in attendance. Since our last review, the family have adhered closely to dietetic guidance provided by our paediatric dietetics team, including a structured ketogenic plan and regular monitoring of ketone levels at home. They report improved daytime alertness, steadier mood and better sleep consolidation. Importantly, Liam Carter has been seizure‑free since 29/09/2017. This represents a clear improvement compared with earlier in the year: in February she had five seizures during sleep and three while awake, and in March she had two in sleep and three while awake, prior to the sustained improvement that followed dietary optimisation and education around trigger avoidance. 

There have been no hospital admissions, rescue medication has not been required, and there are no reported adverse effects from the dietary regimen aside from occasional constipation, managed with increased fluid intake and fibre as advised by dietetics. School attendance has improved and the family feel confident with the current safety plan. 

Examination today was unremarkable. Observations were stable and neurological examination was non‑focal. Current medications are unchanged and tolerability remains good. We have reinforced adherence to the dietary plan, hydration, sleep regularity, and avoidance of known triggers. The family will continue to maintain a seizure diary; they understand to contact us if any breakthrough events occur. 

Plan: 
- Continue current management and dietetic follow‑up.  
- No changes to medication at present given sustained stability.  
- Routine review in six months, earlier if seizures recur.  

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify current status: The letter states, "Importantly, Liam Carter has been seizure‑free since 29/09/2017." Clinic date is 05 July 2018. Calculate duration seizure-free from 29 Sep 2017 to 05 Jul 2018. Months: Oct (1), Nov (2), Dec (3), Jan (4), Feb (5), Mar (6), Apr (7), May (8), Jun (9), plus 5 days into July ≈ 9 months+. This exceeds the 6‑month criterion, so classify as seizure free for 9 months.
